description: Machine-readable banned words and sentence patterns for anti-AI scanning. Source: anti-ai-rules.md

banned_words_context_sensitive.note: banned as a verb meaning 'to emphasize'. OK as noun (a highlight of the game). Flag verb usage.
banned as adjective meaning 'important' (key insight, key lesson). OK as noun (car key) or in proper nouns.
banned in figurative sense (rich history, rich tapestry). OK as literal (rich food, rich person).
banned when it means 'is' (this represents a major shift = this is a major shift). OK in literal sense.
banned in figurative sense (navigate challenges, navigate the landscape). OK in literal sense (navigate the road).

banned_patterns.name: em_dash
banned_patterns.description: Em dash (—) anywhere in content. Replace with period + new sentence, or comma.
banned_patterns.example_bad: The team launched it — and it worked.
banned_patterns.fix: The team launched it. It worked.

banned_patterns.name: tacked_on_ing_clause
banned_patterns.description: Sentence ending with a present participle phrase that adds false significance. The -ing phrase annotates rather than adds meaning.
banned_patterns.example_bad: The team launched the product, demonstrating their commitment to innovation.
banned_patterns.fix: The team launched the product. [If significance is real, state it separately with evidence.]

banned_patterns.name: false_significance_testament
banned_patterns.description: Asserting significance instead of proving it with a fact.
banned_patterns.example_bad: This is a testament to their enduring commitment.
banned_patterns.fix: State the specific fact that proves significance instead.

banned_patterns.name: false_significance_underscores
banned_patterns.description: Using 'underscores' to assert importance without evidence.
banned_patterns.example_bad: This underscores the importance of data.
banned_patterns.fix: State the specific fact that proves importance instead.

banned_patterns.name: false_significance_reflects
banned_patterns.description: Using 'reflects broader trends' or similar to assert significance.
banned_patterns.example_bad: This reflects broader trends in the industry.
banned_patterns.fix: Name the specific trend with evidence, or cut the observation.

banned_patterns.name: not_only_but_also
banned_patterns.description: The 'not only X but also Y' pattern. Sounds nuanced, says nothing extra.
banned_patterns.example_bad: Not only does this save time, but it also reduces costs.
banned_patterns.fix: Say the actual thing directly: 'This saves time and reduces costs.'

banned_patterns.name: vague_attribution_experts
banned_patterns.description: Attributing claims to unnamed 'experts' without a specific source.
banned_patterns.example_bad: Experts argue that AI will transform the industry.
banned_patterns.fix: Name the specific expert and their claim, or own the claim yourself.

banned_patterns.name: vague_attribution_studies
banned_patterns.example_bad: Studies show that this approach increases revenue.
banned_patterns.fix: Name the specific study, or own the claim with 'In my experience...' if it's observation-based.

banned_patterns.name: serves_as
banned_patterns.description: Using 'serves as' instead of 'is'. Copulative avoidance.
banned_patterns.example_bad: Gallery 825 serves as LAAA's exhibition space.

banned_patterns.name: stands_as
banned_patterns.description: Using 'stands as' instead of 'is'. Copulative avoidance.
banned_patterns.example_bad: This stands as the best example of...
banned_patterns.fix: This is the best example of...

banned_patterns.name: title_case_headers
banned_patterns.description: Title case in markdown headers. Sentence case only.
banned_patterns.example_bad: ## Why Your Content Strategy Is Failing
banned_patterns.fix: ## Why your content strategy is failing

banned_patterns.name: generic_opener_today
banned_patterns.regex: ^(?:In today's|In the modern|In an increasingly|In a world where|As we navigate)
banned_patterns.example_bad: In today's fast-paced world, content is changing.
banned_patterns.fix: Start with the specific thing itself — a fact, a moment, a counter-intuitive claim.

banned_patterns.name: have_you_ever_hook
banned_patterns.description: Opening with 'Have you ever...' — a generic hook that belongs to everyone.
banned_patterns.example_bad: Have you ever wondered why content fails?
banned_patterns.fix: Start with the answer, not the question.

banned_patterns.name: as_a_professional_hook
banned_patterns.description: Opening with 'As a [professional/founder/leader]...' — a self-labeling opener.
banned_patterns.example_bad: As a founder, you know the challenges of building.
banned_patterns.fix: Start with the challenge itself, not the label.

notes: hard_ban: Must fix before saving. These are on the NEVER list.
soft_flag: Surface to reviewer but does not block save. Use judgment.
Context-sensitive words require manual review — the scanner flags them as candidates, not definitive violations.
The scanner does not fix violations. It reports them. Claude or the human fixes them.
